jokes.text: What dog did he steal that sweater from? Probably his wife.
jokes.setup: First character asks about someone's ugly sweater using dog comparison
jokes.punchline: Second character escalates by suggesting the man's wife looks like a dog
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke with good economy but relies on basic insult humor

jokes.text: Good God, who shot the couch?
jokes.setup: Will sees an apparently ugly or damaged couch
jokes.punchline: Frames it as if the couch was violently attacked
jokes.explanation: Good visual observation with Will's street perspective on fancy furniture

jokes.text: Give her a break, Hilary. She just won the Kentucky Derby.
jokes.setup: Hilary makes fun of someone's appearance
jokes.punchline: Will compares the woman to a racehorse
jokes.explanation: Will defending someone while still roasting them shows his character nicely

jokes.text: Who's that lady with Nell Carter? That's not Nell Carter. That's my father!
jokes.setup: Someone mistakes Uncle Phil for actress Nell Carter
jokes.punchline: Carlton reveals it's actually his father
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection with Carlton's embarrassed revelation about his father's appearance

jokes.text: God, you're so shallow. I hate you. You're stupid and ugly and I wish you would die. -Sorry. -Okay.
jokes.setup: Hilary calls Ashley shallow
jokes.punchline: Ashley explodes with extreme insults then immediately apologizes casually
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with perfect casual deflation - shows family dynamics

jokes.text: Look at her! Brake! Hit the brake, Will!
jokes.setup: Will is driving a golf cart and sees attractive woman
jokes.punchline: He's so distracted he can't control the cart
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup with Will's hormones causing chaos

jokes.text: I panicked. That squirrel came out of nowhere.
jokes.setup: Will crashed the golf cart
jokes.punchline: Blames it on a squirrel when we know it was because of the woman
jokes.explanation: Will's characteristic way of deflecting blame with absurd excuses

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Mr. Banks, but no golf carts in the dining room.
jokes.setup: The family is in a fancy dining room
jokes.punchline: Manager has to state the obvious rule about golf carts
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of an absurd but necessary rule

jokes.text: I wouldn't be surprised if they revoked our shuffleboard privileges.
jokes.setup: Philip is embarrassed by Will's antics at the country club
jokes.punchline: Worries about losing the most boring possible club privilege
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Philip's priorities and stuffiness

jokes.text: Special bulletin. Hormones to Will.
jokes.setup: Will suddenly notices attractive girl
jokes.punchline: Carlton frames it like a news bulletin about Will's hormones taking over
jokes.explanation: Carlton's dorky but clever way of commenting on Will's obvious attraction

jokes.text: Why do they call him that? Because he never says 'yes.'
jokes.setup: Carlton explains Dr. Mumford is called 'Dr. No'
jokes.punchline: Literal explanation that he never approves of anyone
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup for the character obstacle

jokes.text: Out of here, son. You disgust me.
jokes.setup: Will gives honest but inadequate answers to Dr. Mumford's questions
jokes.punchline: Dr. Mumford's extreme overreaction to normal teenage uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Establishes Dr. Mumford's impossibly high standards through extreme reaction

jokes.text: Boy, you must be on dog food.
jokes.setup: Carlton claims he lacks social graces
jokes.punchline: Will's street-style comeback suggesting Carlton is crazy
jokes.explanation: Classic Will street insult with good economy and character voice

jokes.text: and unequivocally the most flamboyant bachelor since Billy Dee.
jokes.setup: Will boasts about his qualities
jokes.punchline: Claims to be the most flamboyant bachelor since Billy Dee Williams
jokes.explanation: Will's confidence with a good cultural reference that fits his character

jokes.text: Who says the art of conversation is dead?
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton have been saying 'I bet he will/won't' repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Carlton sarcastically comments on their mindless repetition
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke acknowledging the repetitive argument with perfect Carlton wit

jokes.text: Carlton, it's very easy to be a geek. All I have to do is follow you around for a day.
jokes.setup: Carlton offers to teach Will to be a gentleman
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Carlton is the perfect example of being a geek
jokes.explanation: Classic Will/Carlton dynamic with a well-structured insult

jokes.text: It isn't as easy as it looks.
jokes.setup: Will says being a geek is easy
jokes.punchline: Carlton earnestly defends the difficulty of being dorky
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - he takes pride in something others see as negative

jokes.text: Say there, Mimi... might I say that you rate a perfect 10 on my niftiness meter?
jokes.setup: Carlton demonstrates how to talk to a young lady
jokes.punchline: Uses incredibly dorky 'niftiness meter' pickup line
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crafted Carlton line that's both cringey and endearing

jokes.text: I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Carlton delivers his terrible pickup line
jokes.punchline: Will's understated rejection of Carlton's approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in Will's dismissal - says everything with minimal words

jokes.text: Philip, that's what you did on our first date. That's right. You're lucky you got a second one.
jokes.setup: Philip demonstrates his old pickup technique
jokes.punchline: Vivian reveals this was actually his real approach and it barely worked
jokes.explanation: Great marital comedy revealing backstory while deflating Philip's ego

jokes.text: My life was but a mere whisper until you entered into it.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey offers to show proper romantic technique
jokes.punchline: Delivers incredibly flowery, over-the-top romantic speech
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's unexpected romantic eloquence contrasts with his usual sarcasm

jokes.text: Baby!
jokes.setup: Geoffrey delivers elaborate romantic speech
jokes.punchline: Hilary is completely swept away by the butler's charm
jokes.explanation: Perfect single-word reaction showing Geoffrey's unexpected romantic power

jokes.text: Get your own geisha.
jokes.setup: Will wants to learn from Geoffrey too
jokes.punchline: Hilary protectively claims Geoffrey as her own romantic teacher
jokes.explanation: Hilary's selfishness shows through her possessiveness of Geoffrey's attention

jokes.text: By george, I think he's got it. I think he's got it.
jokes.setup: Will correctly identifies the shrimp fork
jokes.punchline: Carlton quotes My Fair Lady about Will's etiquette progress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton cultural reference that fits the teaching situation

jokes.text: Probably off the rack.
jokes.setup: Someone asks where another person got their jacket
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comment about buying ready-to-wear instead of custom clothing
jokes.explanation: Simple class-based snobbishness that establishes the country club atmosphere

jokes.text: Did you hear about his Jaguar? The one he bought secondhand?
jokes.setup: Someone mentions another person's Jaguar
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'scandalous' detail that it's used, not new
jokes.explanation: Continues the theme of country club snobbery with their warped priorities

jokes.text: Bend over. It's Andover. We're going back home.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks where Will transferred from
jokes.punchline: Will makes a crude joke, then Carlton corrects to the actual school name
jokes.explanation: Will can't resist the crude joke even while preparing for his gentleman act

jokes.text: Smithers, sir. Kip Smithers.
jokes.setup: Will needs to give Dr. Mumford a fake preppy name
jokes.punchline: Chooses 'Kip Smithers,' a perfectly generic WASP name
jokes.explanation: The name choice shows Will's idea of what sounds properly preppy

jokes.text: The cutting part.
jokes.setup: Dr. Mumford asks what aspect of thoracic surgery interests Will most
jokes.punchline: Will gives the most basic, obvious answer possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment - cutting through pretense with honest simplicity

jokes.text: That's the part I like, too.
jokes.setup: Will gives simple answer about liking 'the cutting part' of surgery
jokes.punchline: Dr. Mumford agrees with the basic, obvious response
jokes.explanation: Shows that even the sophisticated doctor appreciates Will's honest directness

jokes.text: Really? With turbans and everything?
jokes.setup: Dr. Mumford mentions his string of Arabians (horses)
jokes.punchline: Will thinks he means Arab people and asks about turbans
jokes.explanation: Classic culture clash joke with Will's street perspective on fancy horse talk

jokes.text: Very funny, Smithers. You had me there for a moment. I thought you were a blithering idiot.
jokes.setup: Will makes the turban comment
jokes.punchline: Dr. Mumford thinks Will was making a clever joke, not showing ignorance
jokes.explanation: Great irony - Will's genuine confusion is mistaken for sophisticated humor

jokes.text: Gosh, sir. There's a big question mark on that one.
jokes.setup: Dr. Mumford asks if Will wants to meet his daughter
jokes.punchline: Will tries to play hard-to-get with overly formal awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Will's attempt at preppy coyness comes across as awkward but endearing

jokes.text: I am sick of you white-washed, preppy stuffed shirts.
jokes.setup: Will has been playing the perfect preppy gentleman
jokes.punchline: Mimi reveals she hates exactly the type he's pretending to be
jokes.explanation: Perfect plot twist that undermines Will's entire strategy while revealing character

jokes.text: Someone from the streets.
jokes.setup: Mimi explains what she really wants in a guy
jokes.punchline: She wants exactly what Will naturally is but he's hiding it
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony that drives home the episode's theme about being yourself

jokes.text: Yo, baby. Your prince is in effect, baby.
jokes.setup: Will realizes Mimi wants a street guy
jokes.punchline: Immediately drops the act and goes into full Fresh Prince mode
jokes.explanation: Will Smith's signature delivery showing his natural charisma breaking through

jokes.text: That was the worst homeboy act I've ever heard.
jokes.setup: Will switches to his natural street persona
jokes.punchline: Mimi thinks his real personality is a fake act
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double irony - his authentic self is seen as the worst fake version

jokes.text: He's Kip Smithers, from Connecticut. He transferred from Andover to Bel-Air, so he could row with the crew.
jokes.setup: Will begs Carlton to reveal his real identity
jokes.punchline: Carlton sticks to the fake preppy backstory instead of Will's real street origins
jokes.explanation: Carlton's obliviousness perfectly frustrates Will's attempt to reveal the truth

jokes.text: England?
jokes.setup: Will desperately asks Carlton to tell Mimi where he's really from
jokes.punchline: Carlton guesses England instead of West Philadelphia
jokes.explanation: Carlton's complete cluelessness delivered with perfect comic timing

jokes.text: For future reference, Will, our club frowns on strangulation.
jokes.setup: Will presumably chokes Carlton in frustration
jokes.punchline: Philip casually mentions the country club's anti-strangulation policy
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery treating violence as merely a social etiquette issue

jokes.text: I finally found the perfect pair of alligator pumps... to wear to the Save the Everglades rally tonight.
jokes.setup: Hilary mentions going to an environmental rally
jokes.punchline: She's wearing alligator leather shoes to save the Everglades (where alligators live)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary hypocrisy showing her complete lack of environmental awareness

jokes.text: That fat girl?
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Will likes Mimi Mumford
jokes.punchline: Hilary immediately calls her fat
jokes.explanation: Basic mean girl humor that shows Hilary's shallow nature but lacks creativity

jokes.text: Not today. Liposuction.
jokes.setup: Ashley points out Mimi is not fat
jokes.punchline: Hilary casually mentions she's had liposuction
jokes.explanation: Hilary's casual cruelty delivered with perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: Just wave a chili-cheese dog in front of her nose... and see how much of your arm you come back with.
jokes.setup: Will asks about Mimi looking good now
jokes.punchline: Hilary suggests Mimi would attack someone for food
jokes.explanation: Continues Hilary's mean streak with vivid imagery, though relies on tired fat jokes

jokes.text: A sorrier bunch of deadbeats you'll never meet.
jokes.setup: Vivian says she had her share of admirers before marriage
jokes.punchline: Immediately insults her own former dating pool
jokes.explanation: Vivian's self-deprecating humor shows her down-to-earth personality

jokes.text: See, I fooled you.
jokes.setup: Will and Carlton are having their repetitive betting argument again
jokes.punchline: Will breaks the pattern by agreeing with Carlton to confuse him
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that shows Will learning to outmaneuver Carlton

jokes.text: You went to Penn State. I went to the state pen.
jokes.setup: Carlton misunderstands Will's fake criminal backstory
jokes.punchline: Will corrects with the wordplay between Penn State and state penitentiary
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that gets better on repeat, perfectly delivered by Will

jokes.text: Sorry. I thought Penn State was bad enough.
jokes.setup: Will corrects Carlton about going to state prison, not Penn State
jokes.punchline: Carlton casually insults Penn State while missing the prison reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment showing his academic snobbery while being completely oblivious

jokes.text: K-I-P. It stands for conceived in prison.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains Will's street name 'Kip'
jokes.punchline: Creates ridiculous backstory that it's an acronym for being conceived in prison
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation of Will's fake criminal backstory

jokes.text: No, baby. You misunderstood. He said I wrote for the 2 Live Crew.
jokes.setup: Mimi mentions Will said he rode crew
jokes.punchline: Carlton 'corrects' that he wrote for controversial rap group 2 Live Crew
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that fits the street persona they're creating

jokes.text: But, see, they kicked me out because my lyrics was too abrasive.
jokes.setup: Carlton says Will wrote for 2 Live Crew
jokes.punchline: Will claims he was too extreme for a group famous for explicit lyrics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - claiming to be too extreme for 2 Live Crew shows Will's street credibility

jokes.text: Baby. You look so good... I wish I could plant you and grow a whole field of y'all.
jokes.setup: Will is trying to impress Mimi with street charm
jokes.punchline: Uses agricultural metaphor as a pickup line
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith charm with creative metaphor that became quotable

jokes.text: Baby. You look so good... I wish I could plant you and grow a whole field of y'all.
jokes.setup: Will trying to be smooth with pickup line
jokes.punchline: Agricultural metaphor for attraction
jokes.explanation: Creative pickup line with agricultural metaphor, fits Will's style of creative street talk

jokes.text: Yo, baby, Fresh Prince in full effect. It's time to get busy. You can't hay, can you, baby?
jokes.setup: Will trying to dance and be smooth
jokes.punchline: His dancing is apparently terrible, asking if she can 'hay' (dance)
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment, though the wordplay on 'hay' is unclear and somewhat forced

jokes.text: This music is def, exceptionally def.
jokes.setup: Will is trying to sound street at the formal dance
jokes.punchline: Uses 90s slang 'def' at a stuffy country club event
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's cultural code-switching in an inappropriate setting

jokes.text: I told him if he don't stay out my face, we would take it to the street.
jokes.setup: Mimi asking what Will told her father
jokes.punchline: Will claiming he threatened the respected doctor
jokes.explanation: Standard tough-guy posturing, fits the street persona Will is maintaining

jokes.text: I wasn't listening to him, baby. I was stealing his wallet.
jokes.setup: Mimi asks what Will was talking to her father about
jokes.punchline: Will claims he was robbing Dr. Mumford during the conversation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's fake criminal persona to impress Mimi

jokes.text: Okay, baby. This one I got banned in Buffalo for.
jokes.setup: Mimi asks Will to rap some of his abrasive lyrics
jokes.punchline: Will claims his rap was so extreme it got banned in an entire city
jokes.explanation: Sets up anticipation for Will's 'extreme' rap with absurd claim about Buffalo

jokes.text: And that is just the type of music we will not let our children listen to.
jokes.setup: Will presumably performs his 'banned' rap
jokes.punchline: Dr. Mumford's conservative reaction reveals Will's rap was actually tame
jokes.explanation: Good payoff suggesting Will's 'extreme' rap was probably very mild

jokes.text: Who put this on my head?
jokes.setup: Dr. Mumford asks about Will's ridiculous hat
jokes.punchline: Will acts confused about how the hat got on his head
jokes.explanation: Visual gag with Will's confusion about his own costume choices

jokes.text: I'm Will Smith from West Philly... and I've been busting my butt all night trying to impress you... and trying to scare you.
jokes.setup: Will finally gives up all the pretenses
jokes.punchline: Admits he's been working hard at contradictory goals all night
jokes.explanation: Great character moment where Will acknowledges the absurdity of his situation

jokes.text: baby, ain't no girl that fly for me to go through all this trouble for.
jokes.setup: Will is exhausted from trying to be someone he's not
jokes.punchline: Declares no woman is worth sacrificing his authentic self
jokes.explanation: Strong character growth moment with Will's authentic voice shining through

jokes.text: Wasn't he gorgeous?
jokes.setup: Hilary describes the serious environmental event being grim and sobering
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to gushing about Tom Cruise's looks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment showing her shallow priorities even at serious events

jokes.text: Well, then her husband came in.
jokes.setup: Will excitedly tells Geoffrey about succeeding with a woman by being himself
jokes.punchline: Reveals the encounter was cut short by her husband's arrival
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates Will's moment of triumph with reality
